headlines.title: BRAVES TAKE LEAD AFTER GAME FIGHT
headlines.subtitle: Boston Braves Going Good Because Youth Is Supreme
headlines.fullText: After one of the gamest uphill fights that baseball has ever experienced, the Boston Braves, under the command of George Stallings have reached the top of the National League percentage col- umn. Two months ago this same Boston team was submerged in the depths of oblivion at the very bottom of the heap. And then came the rejuvena- tion. One day in July the Braves won only a passing thought, as every team is expected to win once in a while. But the Braves did not quit. Day in and day out it was the same old story, the Braves won again. Even the most rabid partisan did not think that the spurt would continue for so long a period, but it did, and today Stallings and his men are be- ing freely picked as the team that will meet the Athletics for the world's championship. When you stop to consider the work of the Braves it is really remarkable. Aside from Evers and Maranville not one of the entire outfit was ever con- sidered a star before the present sea- son. There is but one answer. The Braves are all youngsters with amb !- tion and youth must he served. To use a slang expression, the entire slune recht danger of Beston outfits Thebest member of the team that is causing Medraw so much anguish is 34. The veteran Brave Is Fred Mitchell, and he never plays, his duty being to warm up and instruct .The average age of the Braves in a . Mitchell who 18 34. Otto Hess, who will be 34 when the next world- semelle being run off #1 last. The baby of the team is Paut Strand. He will not reach his major- Ity until Dec. 19. 1915. All of the Braves save one were born in the United States. The for- eign-born athlete is Otto Hess, Berne, Switzerland, was where he first saw the light of day, Manager StalinEn now 46 years old, Is a native of Aur the only Boston-born man connected with the outfit. Two of the Braves are born In Springfield and Mitchell In Cambridge. Of the other players, four onme from New York, two from Penn- umane emo from Ohja, two from Texas, two from California and one each form South Carolina, Maryland, North Carolina, Tennessee, Louisiana, Washington, Nebraand, new name: Davers atx are Ings' players are Easterners, six ars Westerners and seven are Southerners.

headlines.title: LOOP ROUTE TO LAKE WORTH IS PLAN OF CLUB

headlines.fullText: Lake Worth an Fort Worth's newest "show place" will be made more ac- cessible by a tuog drive if a move- ment started by the Fort Worth Auto- mobile Club at its annual meeting on Wednesday night is carried through successfully By co-operation of the county com- missioners, the city government, pri- vate citizens and the automobile club, It is hoped that the loop drive may be At the meeting Wednesday night it was suggested that the club's proposed the reservoir, but no action was tak- n on this suggestion One of the proposed routes to Lake Worth is west on West Seventh street. weat on the Arlington Heights boule- ward to the Greenwood cemetery car line, north alongside the Greenvoo] car line to the White Settlement road, west on the White settlement road -09 yards beyond country bar na wrest from the schoolhouse to the Ten trong Twentieth street to bluff, and down hill to Ohle gardens continuing west to Castleberry's schoolhouse and thence on the same road to the reservoir as over the first proposed route. headlines.title: EIGHT SURVIVE FOR NATIONAL GOLF HONORS

headlines.title: CLOSE GAMES EXPECTED IN ELKS' LEAGUE
headlines.fullText: Bowling will be resumed in the Elkes' League tonight when the second sertes of games will be inaugurated on the Eiks' alleys. Friday is the regular league night, but because of the bar- becue and dance to be given by the Elks at Lake Erie Friday night, the Bowling schedule was moved up one night. for this week only. The Braves continue to lead the league with a clean percentage mark. tonight in Gutfinis bryant's busters The Bushers have proven themselves league, but expect to kill the jinx by taking two straight from the league len ders Preliminary to the big attraction, the Panthers and Athletics, who are tied the Rushers for second place, win roll two games. Roth teams have been strentghened and expect to be in the thick of the fight for the pennant from now on. The game between the Pan- thers and Athletics will begin prompt- ly at 7:20.
